service: Prosecute juvenile delinquency cases in Family Court.
oneLiner: The Law Department, not a District Attorney's office, prosecutes juvenile delinquency cases because New York routes children accused of law-breaking into a separate Family Court system focused on rehabilitation rather than the adult criminal courts.
summary: New York State law treats most alleged offenses by children under a certain age as juvenile delinquency matters handled in Family Court rather than criminal prosecutions in adult court. The City's Law Department serves as the "presentment agency" in these cases — the entity that brings and argues the case against the young person — working alongside the Family Court's broader mandate to weigh rehabilitation and family welfare alongside accountability.

sources.label: New York City Law Department — Wikipedia
